medications.name: Lisinopril
medications.instructions: Take in the AM. HELD on the morning of surgery (6/10) and perioperatively per protocol; resume when stable and euvolemic.
medications.notes: One of three antihypertensive agents; BP remains above goal. Held perioperatively.

medications.name: Amlodipine
medications.notes: Added when BP uncontrolled on lisinopril alone.

medications.name: Hydrochlorothiazide
medications.instructions: Take in the AM. HELD perioperatively; resume with PCP follow-up.
medications.notes: Third agent added 2021; BP still frequently 150-168 systolic on home log.

medications.name: Metformin
medications.instructions: Take with meals. HELD perioperatively and around contrast imaging; inpatient glycemic control with sliding-scale insulin. Resume when tolerating diet and renal function confirmed at baseline.

medications.name: Atorvastatin
medications.notes: LDL at goal. Note potential interaction profile to consider if a DOAC is later started.

medications.name: Omeprazole
medications.notes: Chronic. Relevant for GI protection if antithrombotic therapy is initiated.

medications.name: Fish oil (omega-3)
medications.instructions: OTC. Disclose to any provider considering antithrombotic therapy (mild platelet effect at high doses).

medications.name: Piperacillin-tazobactam
medications.indication: Perforated cholecystitis with localized biliary peritonitis (empiric broad-spectrum coverage)
medications.instructions: Inpatient IV. Intraoperative bile culture grew pansensitive E. coli; continuing per surgery. Planned ~4-5 day post-source-control course; transition to oral as appropriate.
medications.notes: Sulfa allergy on file; no penicillin allergy. Tolerating without reaction.

medications.name: Enoxaparin (prophylactic dose)
medications.instructions: PROPHYLACTIC dose only (not therapeutic). Sequential compression devices also in use. Distinct from any therapeutic anticoagulation that might be considered for atrial fibrillation.
medications.notes: Started POD#1 once surgical hemostasis confirmed. This is VTE prophylaxis, NOT stroke-prevention anticoagulation for AF.

medications.name: Oxycodone
medications.instructions: Use sparingly; tapering as pain improves. Patient using minimal amounts by POD#3-4.

medications.name: Insulin aspart (sliding scale)
medications.indication: Inpatient glycemic management while metformin held
medications.instructions: Correctional scale. Resume home metformin at discharge when tolerating diet and renal function at baseline.

reviewedBy: Dr. Priya Anand (PGY-2) + Inpatient Pharmacy (POD#4 medication reconciliation)
_anticoagulation_note: IMPORTANT: The patient is on NO therapeutic anticoagulant and NO antiplatelet agent at baseline or currently. The only parenteral agent on the list (enoxaparin 40 mg SubQ daily) is PROPHYLACTIC-dose VTE prophylaxis, not stroke-prevention anticoagulation. Any therapeutic anticoagulation for the new atrial fibrillation would be a new decision.
